facts.text: George Washington once stopped in Florida, New York, staying the night at Baird's Tavern during his 1783 journey through the town.
The Hudson River Chain, a massive iron barrier that kept British ships at bay, was forged at Sterling Iron Works in nearby Warwick.
Every summer, the Hudson Valley Jazz Festival—originally called the Warwick Valley Jazz Festival—draws musicians and fans to Florida's streets.
Polka legend Jimmy Sturr, a trumpeter and saxophonist, grew up in Florida and still leads his celebrated orchestra from here.
The village’s name, Florida, was chosen by early settlers in the 1700s, reflecting the region’s lush, tropical‑like scenery.
After closing in 2011, the Mid‑Orange Correctional Facility’s grounds were converted into Wickham Woodlands Park, a public green space centered on Wickham Lake.
